title: Down The River
content:
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve done with joys anï؟½ misery,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ why should I repine?
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s no one knows the past but me
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ that olï؟½ dog oï؟½ mine.
آ آ آ آ We camp anï؟½ walk anï؟½ camp anï؟½ walk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ find it fairly good;
آ آ آ آ He can do anything but talk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ he wouldnï؟½t if he could.

آ آ آ آ We sits anï؟½ thinks beside the fire,
آ آ آ آ With all the stars a-shine,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ no one knows our thoughts but me
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ that there dog oï؟½ mine.
آ آ آ آ We has our Johnny-cake anï؟½ ï؟½scrag,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ finds ï؟½em fairly good;
آ آ آ آ He can do anything but talk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ he wouldnï؟½t if he could.

آ آ آ آ He gets a ï؟½possum now anï؟½ then,
آ آ آ آ I cooks it on the fire;
آ آ آ آ He has his water, me my tea,
آ آ آ آ What more could we desire?
آ آ آ آ He gets a rabbit when he likes,
آ آ آ آ We finds it pretty good;
آ آ آ آ He can do anything but talk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ he wouldnï؟½t if he could.

آ آ آ آ I has me smoke, he has his rest,
آ آ آ آ When sunsetï؟½s gettinï؟½ dim;
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ if I do get drunk at times,
آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s all the same to him.
آ آ آ آ So longï؟½s heï؟½s got me swag to mind,
آ آ آ آ He thinks that times is good;
آ آ آ آ He can do anything but talk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ he wouldnï؟½t if he could.

آ آ آ آ He gets his tucker from the cook,
آ آ آ آ For cook is good to him,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ when I sobers up a bit,
آ آ آ آ He goes anï؟½ has a swim.
آ آ آ آ He likes the rivers where I fish,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ all the world is good;
آ آ آ آ He can do anything but talk,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ he wouldnï؟½t if he could.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry Lawson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Henry Lawson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
